revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天石門別神]][[zh:天石門別神]]
{{Infobox deity | Name = Amanoiwatowake no Kami | Generation name = | Predecessor= | Successor= | Kami classification = {{ill|Kami|lt=Kami|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|ja|天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神|6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | Full name = Amanoiwatowake Yasukunidama no Mikoto | Other names = Amanoiwatowake Yasukunidama no Mikoto, Tamano no Mikoto, Ōkunisu no Tamano no Mikoto, {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|qid=Q849412|lt=Amano-Tajikarao|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} (Ōtachi-Karano no Mikoto), Kushi-Iwamado no Kami, Toyo-Iwamado no Kami, Amano-Iwatsu-Yamato-I no Mikoto | Alias = | Divine Rank = | Divine aspect = Guardian of the Gates (御門) | Mausoleum = | Father = {{ill|Tenrai no Mikoto|ja|天雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenrai no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Mother = | Relatives = | Spouse = | Children = {{ill|Ameno-Oshihi|ja|アメノオシヒ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno-Oshihi|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10939616|1=Ama-no-Oshihi|2=ja|3=アメノオシヒ|4=zh|5=天忍日命}} (Amano-Oshihi no Mikoto), {{ill|Kotonomachihime no Mikoto|ja|許等能麻知媛命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotonomachihime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kotonomachihime no Mikoto) | Shrine = | Shrines worshipping = {{ill|Asakura Shrine|ja|朝椋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakura Shrine|qq=}}, {{ill|Kuzu Shrine|ja|九頭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuzu Shrine|1=Kuzu Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11370471}}, {{ill|Amanoiwatowake Shrine|ja|天石門別神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoiwatowake Shrine|1=Amanoiwatowake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60985333}}, etc. | Related clans = {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|qid=Q17227938|lt=Otomo clan|1=Ōtomo clan (ancient)}} (Otomo no Muraji), {{ill|Maruko clan|ja|丸子氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maruko clan|1=Maruko clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11368098}} (Maruko no Muraji), {{ill|Saeki Clan|ja|佐伯氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki clan|qq=}} (Saeki no Muraji), {{ill|Hihafuri clan|ja|日奉氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihafuri clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 西党|1=Hihafuri clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11627255}} (Hihafuri no Muraji), {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}} }} '''Amanoiwatowake no Kami''' (天石門別神) is a {{ill|Kami|en|Kami|qid=Q524158|lt=Kami (Shinto)|1=Kami}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|qid=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}. He is considered the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|qid=Q17227938|lt=Otomo clan|1=Ōtomo clan (ancient)}}, but deities with the same name also appear as ancestral gods of the [[Inbe clan]] in the two branches Awa Imibe (阿波忌部) and Awa Imibe (安房忌部) clans. Thus, "Amanoiwatowake no Mikoto" may refer to two distinct deities (see the [[#Genealogy|Genealogy]] section below). The deity appears in the segment of the {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|Tenson kōrin|qid=Q1084733|lt=Tenson Kōrin|1=Tenson kōrin}} in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}. It is written that {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}}, upon descending from heaven, was accompanied by the {{ill|Imperial Regalia of Japan|en|Imperial Regalia of Japan|qid=Q327465|lt=Three Sacred Treasures|1=Imperial Regalia of Japan}}, {{ill|Tokoyo Omoikane no Kami|ja|常世思金神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokoyo Omoikane no Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オモイカネ|1=Omoikane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q586088}}, {{ill|Ameno Tajikarao|ja|天手力男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno Tajikarao|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノタヂカラオ|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q849412}}, and Tenshimonbekami. In this segment, Tenshimonbekami is also called '''Kushi-iwamado no Kami''' (櫛石窓神) or '''Toyoiwamado no Kami''' (豊石窓神), and is described as a gate deity. Although many deities appearing in the Tenson Kōrin are also featured in the earlier {{ill|Iwatogakure|ja|岩戸隠れ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatogakure|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3231835}}, this deity does not appear there. Tenshimonbekami has traditionally been enshrined at the four gates of the Imperial Palace. In the western section of the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|qid=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities|1=Department of Divinities}}, it was revered as the gate deity and listed among the shrines of Imperial rites. Tenshimonbekami is sometimes described as a child of {{ill|Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja|天太玉神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Kaguyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Overview == Amanoiwatowake no Kami appears in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'' during the episode of {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|Tenson kōrin|qid=Q1084733|lt=Tenson Kōrin|1=Tenson kōrin}}. When {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|en|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q1056017|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|1=Ninigi-no-Mikoto}} descended to earth, he was accompanied by the {{ill|Imperial Regalia of Japan|en|Imperial Regalia of Japan|qid=Q327465|lt=Three Sacred Treasures|1=Imperial Regalia of Japan}}, along with {{ill|Omoikane|en|Omoikane|qid=Q586088|lt=Tokoyo-Omoikane no Kami|1=Omoikane}} (Tokoyo Omoikane no Kami), {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|qid=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} (Amano-Tajikarao no Kami), and Amanoiwatowake no Kami. In this passage, '''Amanoiwatowake no Kami''' is also called '''Amanoiwato-Wake no Kami''' (天石戸別神) and is given the alternative names: * '''Kushi-Iwamado no Kami''' (櫛石窓神) * '''Toyo-Iwamado no Kami''' (豊石窓神) These names refer to his role as a '''guardian deity of gates''' (御門). Many of the deities appearing in the Tenson Kōrin episode also appear in the {{ill|Amano-Iwato|ja|岩戸隠れ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amano-Iwato|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天岩戸}} (Cave Concealment) episode, but '''Amanoiwatowake no Kami does not appear in Amano-Iwato'''. Since ancient times, '''Amanoiwatowake no Kami has been enshrined at the four gates of the imperial palace''' as a guardian deity. Within the {{ill|Department of Divinities|en|Department of Divinities|qid=Q2264720|lt=Department of Divinities|1=Department of Divinities}} (Ministry of Divinities), he was worshipped at the '''Western Court''' as a '''divine protector of the palace gates''', making him one of the '''Shikinaisha (式内社, government-registered shrines)'''. He is also sometimes considered the '''child of Futodama-no-Kami (天太玉神)'''. In the ''{{ill|Kogo Shūi|en|Kogo Shūi|qid=Q531555|lt=Kogo Shūi|1=Kogo Shūi}}'', '''Toyoiwamado-no-Mikoto (豊磐間戸命) and Kushi-Iwamado-no-Mikoto (櫛磐間戸命)''' are mentioned as deities who '''guarded the doors of the divine palace'''. Similarly, in the ''{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}'', they are listed as '''gods enshrined by the Mikado priests (御門巫, Mikado no Ko)'''. The ''Shijisai-shiki'' (四時祭式) mentions a '''ritual performed in May and December at the four gates of the imperial palace''', believed to be the '''festival for Toyoiwamado-no-Mikoto and Kushi-Iwamado-no-Mikoto'''. The ''Norito-shiki'' (祝詞式) also includes a prayer for this festival, stating that the '''two deities guard the palace gates diligently, morning and evening'''. Additionally, it specifies that '''each of the four gates has two of these guardian deities'''. == Genealogy == According to the ''Furuya-ke Keifu'' (古屋家家譜, "Furuya Family Genealogy"), '''Amanoiwatowake Yasukunidama no Mikoto (天石門別安国玉命)''' was the '''third-generation descendant of {{ill|Amenokaminari no Mikoto|ja|天雷命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenokaminari no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (安牟須比命)''', a child of '''Takamimusubi-no-Mikoto (高皇産霊尊)''' and the ancestor of the Otomo clan. In this genealogy, he is also called: * '''Amanoiwatowake Yasukunidama no Mikoto (天石門別安国玉主命)''' * '''Ōkunisu-Tama-no-Mikoto (大国栖玉命)''' * '''Ōtachi-Karano-no-Mikoto (大刀辛雄命)''', another name for '''Amano-Tajikarao-no-Kami (天手力男神)''' == Shrines Worshipping Amanoiwatowake no Kami == * {{ill|Kushi-Iwamado Shrine|ja|櫛石窓神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushi-Iwamado Shrine|1=Kushi iwamado Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17218980}} ([[:en:Tanba-Sasayama|Tanba-Sasayama]], {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}) * {{ill|Taisai Amanoiwato Hiko Shrine|ja|大祭天石門彦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taisai Amanoiwato Hiko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Hamada, Shimane|en|Hamada, Shimane|qid=Q432055|lt=Hamada|1=Hamada, Shimane}}, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|qid=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture|1=Shimane Prefecture}}) * {{ill|Amanoiwato Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社 (福知山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoiwato Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|qq=}} ({{ill|Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|en|Fukuchiyama, Kyoto|qid=Q3355306|lt=Fukuchiyama|1=Fukuchiyama, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}) * {{ill|Tenshaku Shrine|ja|天石立神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshaku Shrine|1=Ama-no-Iwatate Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11443184}} ({{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|qid=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}) * {{ill|Otani Shrine|ja|大谷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otani Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Kami, Kōchi|en|Kami, Kōchi|qid=Q877689|lt=Kami|1=Kami, Kōchi}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|qid=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}) * {{ill|Amanoiwatowake Yakura-hime Shrine|ja|天石門別八倉比売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoiwatowake Yakura-hime Shrine|1=Yakurahime Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11390577}} - Arrow Shrine (摂社箭執神社) ({{ill|Tokushima (city)|en|Tokushima (city)|qid=Q207309|lt=Tokushima|1=Tokushima (city)}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|qid=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}) * {{ill|Amanoiwatowake Shrine|ja|天石門別神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoiwatowake Shrine|1=Amanoiwatowake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60985333}} ({{ill|Ibaraki Shrine|ja|茨木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Shrine|1=Ibaraki Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11617924}} Okumiya) ({{ill|Ibaraki, Osaka|en|Ibaraki, Osaka|qid=Q502403|lt=Ibaraki|1=Ibaraki, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}) *{{ill|Kushi-iwamado Shrine|ja|櫛石窓神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kushi-iwamado Shrine|1=Kushi iwamado Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17218980}} (Tanba Sasayama, Hyōgo Prefecture) * {{ill|Daisai Tenshimon Hiko Shrine|ja|大祭天石門彦神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daisai Tenshimon Hiko Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Hamada, Shimane Prefecture) * {{ill|Amanoiwato Shrine|en|Amanoiwato Shrine|qid=Q2841102|lt=Amaiwato Shrine|1=Amanoiwato Shrine}} (Fukuchiyama, Kyoto Prefecture) * {{ill|Amaiwato Shrine|ja|天石立神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaiwato Shrine|1=Ama-no-Iwatate Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11443184}} (Nara, Nara Prefecture) * {{ill|Ōtani Shrine|ja|大谷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtani Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Kami, Kochi Prefecture) * {{ill|Tenshimon Yakura Shrine|ja|天石門別八倉比売神社摂社箭執神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshimon Yakura Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Tokushima, Tokushima Prefecture) * {{ill|Tenshimon Shrine|ja|天石門別神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshimon Shrine|1=Amanoiwatowake Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60985333}} (Ibaraki Shrine, Osaka Prefecture) * {{ill|Hakken Shrine|ja|八剱神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakken Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八剣神社|1=Yatsurugi Shrine (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11390593}} (Gifu Prefecture) * {{ill|Tajikarao Shrine|ja|手力雄神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tajikarao Shrine|qq=}} (Gifu Prefecture)
== See Also == * {{ill|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|en|Ōtomo clan (ancient)|qid=Q17227938|lt=Ōtomo Clan|1=Ōtomo clan (ancient)}} * {{ill|Tenson kōrin|en|Tenson kōrin|qid=Q1084733|lt=Tenson kōrin|1=Tenson kōrin}} == References == {{Reflist|2|qq=}} == Bibliography == * Hogata, Toshio, "Comprehensive Lineage of Ancient Clans," 1986. * {{Cite book|和書|title=神道大辞典（縮刷版）|publisher=[[Rinsen Shoten]]|year=1969|editor=[[Miyaji Naoichi]], [[Saeki Ariyoshi|qq=}} * {{translated page|ja|天石門別神|version=103210044|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:天石門別神]]
